advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Pediatric nutritional requirements vary significantly across developmental stages: infant, toddler, school-age, and adolescent. Adequate iron, calcium, vitamin D, and omega-3s are critical for neurological and skeletal development.
research_findings: Documented age-specific DRI tables for 18 nutrients across 4 pediatric age groups. Identified that iron-deficiency anemia affects approximately 20% of toddlers globally, making targeted supplementation protocols essential.
current_focus_areas: Developing age-stratified pediatric meal planning protocols; building nutritional assessment tools for growth monitoring; coordinating with clinical teams on failure-to-thrive dietary interventions.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Lifecycle & Specialized Population Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 21 (Food Texture Analysis). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 39%.
Progress update: Completed DRI-based nutritional assessment frameworks for 4 pediatric age groups. Validated iron supplementation protocols for toddler population. Progress advanced to 44%.
Phase transition: advanced from 'independent_learning' to 'collaborative_learning'. Bot 11 now entering collaborative learning phase. Progress: 52%.

purpose: Knowledge library resource available for all departments to reference and use
